text: Science has a clearer story about lunar regolith than social media does. Lunar regolith is the layer of loose material covering the Moon's surface. It’s not just dust; it contains tiny glass beads formed by meteoroid impacts. Scientists have studied regolith for clues about the Moon’s history and potential resources. This material could even help build lunar habitats for future explorers. Want to dive deeper into space science? Hit that follow button!
